output.code:
# Let's open the text file to see its raw content
with open('/mnt/data/file-1hpBqL3EcEtvMsnxsK5DWS-text_to_analyse.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
print(text)

# Let's see the length and maybe tail part
print(len(text))
print(text[:200])
print(text[-200:])

# Let's find positions of all double quotes
quote_positions = [i for i, c in enumerate(text) if c == '"']
quote_positions[:10], len(quote_positions)

# Extract the substring between the first two quotes
first_quote = quote_positions[0]
second_quote = quote_positions[1]
raw_span = text[first_quote+1:second_quote]
print("Raw span:", raw_span[:100], "...", raw_span[-100:])
# Trim leading/trailing whitespace and punctuation as per instructions: whitespace, quotes, punctuation ، ؛ : . , ! ? … (English), tatweel (not relevant)
import re
# Trim: remove leading/trailing spaces and characters in regex set
trimmed = raw_span.strip(" \n\t\r\"'()[]{}<>،؛:.,!?…ـ")
print("Trimmed span:", trimmed[:100], "...", trimmed[-100:])
# Confirm raw_text[start:end] equals trimmed
# Find trimmed in raw
start = text.find(trimmed)
end = start + len(trimmed)
print(start, end, text[start:end]==trimmed)

third_quote = quote_positions[2]
raw_span2 = text[third_quote+1:]  # until end
print("Raw2:", raw_span2[:100], "...", raw_span2[-100:])
trimmed2 = raw_span2.strip(" \n\t\r\"'()[]{}<>،؛:.,!?…ـ")
print("Trimmed2:", trimmed2[:100], "...", trimmed2[-100:])
# Find index of trimmed2
start2 = text.find(trimmed2)
end2 = start2 + len(trimmed2)
print(start2, end2, text[start2:end2]==trimmed2)

And they will say, 'Would that we had been with the people of the Book, and had not been with the people of the Fire!' But they will say, 'Indeed, we were wronged, and we were killed in the way of Allah.'
